nuggets.headline: Sounwave steered Kendrick Lamar's 'DAMN.' away from a 'TPAB' sequel
nuggets.text: Producer Sounwave, a central figure in Kendrick Lamar's creative circle for over a decade, was instrumental in the sonic shift on "DAMN.". He intentionally guided the album away from the jazz and funk of its predecessor, stating, "We could’ve easily done another To Pimp A Butterfly but there’s no fun in that." This conscious decision to explore a different energy defined the recording sessions and resulted in the album's harder, more direct sound.

source.title: Sounwave Explains Every Song He Helped Produce On Kendrick Lamar’s DAMN. | The FADER
source.quoteSnippet: “We could’ve easily done another To Pimp A Butterfly but there’s no fun in that, “ he recently told The FADER over the phone from Los Angeles. “We thrive off of pushing ourselves.”

nuggets.imageCaption: Producer Sounwave (left) with Kendrick Lamar during the DAMN. era.

nuggets.headline: "HUMBLE." became Kendrick Lamar's first No. 1 hit as a lead artist
nuggets.text: While Kendrick Lamar had reached the top of the charts as a featured artist on Taylor Swift's "Bad Blood," "HUMBLE." marked a major commercial milestone as his first solo single to hit No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100. It also had the highest debut for a hip-hop song since Eminem's "Love the Way You Lie" in 2010. The track's immediate success reasserted his chart dominance after the more musically complex and less radio-oriented "To Pimp a Butterfly."

source.title: Humble (song)
source.quoteSnippet: "Humble" debuted at number two and later peaked at number one on the US Billboard Hot 100, becoming Lamar's first number one and highest-charting single as a lead artist... It marked the highest debut for a hip hop song since "Love the Way You Lie" by Eminem featuring Rihanna.

nuggets.headline: The DNA of 'DAMN.' connects directly to Tyler, The Creator's sound
nuggets.text: If you appreciate the textures on "DAMN.," turn to Tyler, The Creator. The link is producer Steve Lacy, a member of The Internet, who produced the track "PRIDE." on this very album. Lacy is also a cornerstone of Tyler's sound, contributing heavily to his albums. Start with Tyler's 2019 album "IGOR" to hear Lacy's distinctive production style in a different context.

source.title: A Deep Dive Into How Kendrick Lamar Made 'DAMN.'
source.quoteSnippet: Beneath the surface, there’s a cavalcade of trusted collaborators... and a sprinkling of fresh faces (Zacari, Bekon, Kaytranada, the Internet’s Steve Lacy).

nuggets.imageCaption: Tyler, The Creator's work features producer Steve Lacy, who also produced on DAMN.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar is a Pulitzer Prize-winning artist from Compton, California, whose work consistently redefines the creative and thematic boundaries of hip-hop. His albums are dense, narrative-driven bodies of work that have cemented him as one of the most important and groundbreaking rappers of his generation.
